title_en: Introduction to an-Nawawi's Sharh of Sahih Muslim
content_en:
Yahya ibn Sharaf an-Nawawi stands among the most productive and widely read scholars in the history of Sunni Islam. Born in 631 AH in Nawa, a village in the Hauran region of Syria from which he took his name, he died young — in 676 AH at the age of forty-five — yet left behind a body of work that became foundational across multiple disciplines. His commentary on Sahih Muslim, known formally as al-Minhaj fi Sharh Sahih Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj, is among the most important of these works and remains in print and active use to this day.

What is remarkable about the Sharh is that an-Nawawi produced it relatively early in his career, before he had composed many of the later works for which he became famous. The commentary reflects a scholar in command of his field yet still developing the distinctive voice that would characterize his mature writings. Despite this, the Sharh on Muslim achieved almost immediate recognition as the standard commentary on the collection, a status it has never lost.

Islam ibn al-Hajjaj al-Qushayri al-Naysaburi compiled his Sahih with a different organizational logic than al-Bukhari. While al-Bukhari arranged his collection primarily by legal topic, Muslim organized his hadiths thematically and paid special attention to assembling all relevant chains for a single report in one place. This approach has advantages for the student of hadith transmission but can be less intuitive for the student approaching the collection for its legal rulings. An-Nawawi's commentary navigates this structure clearly, helping readers understand why Muslim grouped certain hadiths together and what the organizational choices imply.

The introduction to the Sharh, known as the Muqaddimah, is itself a substantial piece of scholarship on the hadith sciences. An-Nawawi discusses the definitions of sound (sahih), good (hasan), and weak (da'if) hadiths; the nature of Muslim's specific criteria for inclusion in his collection; and the scholarly debates about whether Sahih al-Bukhari or Sahih Muslim ranks higher in overall reliability. On this last question, he presents the standard position of the Egyptian and Maghrebi scholars — that Muslim is in some ways more technically rigorous in his chain-presentation — while noting that al-Bukhari is generally considered superior in knowledge of the narrators.

An-Nawawi's student years in Damascus placed him in a rich intellectual environment. He studied under many of the leading scholars of Syria in the seventh Islamic century, and the breadth of his reading is evident in the Sharh, where he regularly cites works of Shafi'i jurisprudence, Arabic grammar, and classical scholarship on theological questions. His training was deeply grounded in the Shafi'i legal tradition, though he engaged other schools of thought with characteristic care.

The Sharh on Sahih Muslim became the model for how a hadith commentary should handle the relationship between the text itself and the disciplines — legal, theological, linguistic — that bear on its interpretation. Its influence on the way Muslim students encounter the second most important hadith collection in Sunni Islam is immense and continues undiminished.
title_id: Pengantar Syarh al-Nawawi atas Sahih Muslim
content_id:
Yahya ibn Syaraf al-Nawawi berdiri di antara ulama paling produktif dan paling banyak dibaca dalam sejarah Islam Sunni. Lahir pada 631 H di Nawa, sebuah desa di kawasan Hauran Suriah dari mana beliau mengambil namanya, beliau wafat dalam usia muda — pada 676 H di usia empat puluh lima tahun — namun meninggalkan karya yang menjadi fondasi di berbagai disiplin ilmu. Komentarnya atas Sahih Muslim, yang secara resmi dikenal sebagai Al-Minhaj fi Syarh Sahih Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj, termasuk karya-karya terpenting ini dan hingga hari ini masih dicetak dan digunakan secara aktif.

Yang luar biasa tentang Syarh ini adalah bahwa al-Nawawi menyelesaikannya di bawah tekanan waktu yang besar — beliau mengerjakannya di tengah-tengah tugas mengajar, menulis, ibadah, dan amar ma'ruf yang mengisi hidupnya yang singkat. Meskipun demikian, hasilnya adalah karya yang mencakup empat dimensi analisis yang berdiri sendiri: penjelasan bahasa Arab, uraian teologis, penetapan hukum, dan bio-bibliografi narator. Jarang ada satu penulis yang menguasai semua empat dimensi ini; al-Nawawi melakukannya dalam satu kitab.

Al-Nawawi mendekati Sahih Muslim dengan keyakinan penuh bahwa koleksi hadis ini — bersama Sahih al-Bukhari — merupakan sumber paling otoritatif setelah Al-Qur'an itu sendiri. Metodologinya dalam Syarh mencerminkan keyakinan ini: ia tidak mempertanyakan keaslian hadis-hadis dalam Muslim tetapi bekerja dari kepastian keaslian mereka ke implikasi yang mereka bawa — linguistik, teologis, dan hukum.

Struktur Syarh mengikuti susunan Sahih Muslim: Imam Muslim mengorganisir koleksinya berdasarkan topik-topik hukum dan teologis, dan al-Nawawi memberikan komentar bab demi bab dan hadis demi hadis. Judul-judul bab yang diberikan Muslim kepada setiap kelompok hadis mendapat perhatian tersendiri dari al-Nawawi, karena pilihan judul mencerminkan pemahaman Muslim tentang implikasi hukum dari hadis-hadis dalam setiap kelompok.

Salah satu alasan Syarh tetap tak tergantikan adalah ketidakberadaan karya saingan yang sepadan. Beberapa komentar lain atas Sahih Muslim telah ditulis sebelum dan sesudah al-Nawawi, tetapi tidak ada yang menggabungkan keluasan cakupan, kedalaman analisis linguistik, keluwesan teologis, dan ketelitian yurisprudensinya dalam cara yang sama. Pelajar hadis Sunni mempelajari Syarh al-Nawawi bukan karena kekurangan alternatif tetapi karena tidak ada alternatif yang setara.

Syarh juga mencerminkan kepribadian intelektual al-Nawawi yang khas: rasa hormat mendalam terhadap tradisi yang dipadukan dengan keberanian intelektual untuk tidak setuju ketika bukti menunjuk ke arah lain. Al-Nawawi bukan sekadar transmitter pandangan-pandangan sebelumnya; beliau adalah seorang pemikir mandiri yang pendapatnya pada pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang diperdebatkan diperhitungkan serius oleh para ulama setelahnya. Kombinasi kerendahhatian dan ketegasan intelektual inilah yang menjadikan karyanya begitu dihormati.
content_ar: Yahya ibn Sharaf النووي stands among the most productive and widely read العلماء in the history of Sunni الإسلام. Born in 631 AH in Nawa, a village in the Hauran region of Syria from which he took his name, he died young — in 676 AH at the age of forty-five — yet left behind a الجسد of work that became foundational across multiple disciplines. His commentary on صحيح مسلم, known formally as al-Minhaj fi Sharh صحيح مسلم ibn al-Hajjaj, is among the most المهم of these works and remains in print and active use to this day. What is remarkable about the Sharh is that النووي produced it relatively early in his career, قبل he had composed many of the later works for which he became famous. The commentary reflects a scholar in command of his field yet still developing the distinctive voice that would characterize his mature writings. رغم this, the Sharh on مسلم achieved almost immediate recognition as the standard commentary on the collection, a status it has never lost. الإسلام ibn al-Hajjaj al-Qushayri al-Naysaburi compiled his صحيح with a different organizational logic than البخاري. بينما البخاري arranged his collection primarily by legal topic, مسلم organized his hadiths thematically and paid special attention to assembling all relevant chains for a single report in one place. This approach has advantages for the student of الحديث transmission but can be less intuitive for the student approaching the collection for its legal rulings. النووي's commentary navigates this structure clearly, helping readers understand why مسلم grouped certain hadiths together and what the organizational choices imply. The introduction to the Sharh, known as the Muqaddimah, is itself a substantial piece of scholarship on the الحديث sciences. النووي discusses the definitions of sound (صحيح), الخير (حسن), and weak (ضعيف) hadiths; the nature of مسلم's specific criteria for inclusion in his collection; and the العلمي debates about whether صحيح البخاري or صحيح مسلم ranks higher in overall reliability. On this last question, he presents the standard position of the Egyptian and Maghrebi العلماء — that مسلم is in some ways more technically rigorous in his chain-presentation — بينما noting that البخاري is generally considered superior in العلم of the narrators. النووي's student years in Damascus placed him in a rich intellectual environment. He studied under many of the leading العلماء of Syria in the سابعاً Islamic century, and the breadth of his reading is evident in the Sharh, where he regularly cites works of الشافعي jurisprudence, Arabic grammar, and الكلاسيكي scholarship on theological questions. His training was deeply grounded in the الشافعي legal التراث, though he engaged other schools of thought with characteristic care. The Sharh on صحيح مسلم became the model for how a الحديث commentary should handle the relationship between the text itself and the disciplines
